title: 4: User-friendly protocol: Cost-efficient Primer Exchange Reaction (PER) concatemerization (SABER-FISH)

steps: Add mix to strip tube with 10 µL, 5µM hairpin and mix.
Incubate for 00:15:00 at 37 °C , then pause cycler.
Remove tube from the cycler, add 10 µL, 10µM probe oligos and mix.
Incubate at 37 °C for desired extension time (see Note 2 ).
Mix with the following components On ice : Add the polymerase last. %s %s
Heat to 80 °C 00:20:00 to inactivate the Bst polymerase.

To check the lengths of extensions, load 10 µL, of reaction with loading dye on a dense agarose gel (~ 1.25 % ). You can also load samples into 1 %, E-Gel EX agarose gels (Thermo Fisher G402001). Note 5: We recommend using primary probes extended to ~500-650 nt and branch probes extended to ~250-450 nt. Note 6: Because there aren’t G bases in the concatemer sequences, and they are highly single-stranded, intercalating dyes are not as effective as they are at staining other types of sequences (we recommend using the Sybr Gold dye ). This means the bands may look faint on the gel but still be efficiently elongated, so don’t be surprised if you need to turn up the contrast quite a bit.
While purification of probes is not usually required for cell applications, we have found it helpful for SABER-FISH in tissue.
Purify probes by running 90 µL, of reaction (for 500 nt probe) over one MinElute PCR purification column (Qiagen #28004). Add PB buffer to 7×the volume of reaction being purified.
Determine concentration after purification by nanodrop using ssDNA setting.
creator: Jocelynkishi
